briefing.title: Show HN: IssueScout – Find open source issues worth contributing to
briefing.note: IssueScout going open-source lowers barriers for experimentation and custom deployment. Why it matters: Open access to IssueScout lowers switching cost and pressures competing proprietary tools.

editors_note.headline: This week
editors_note.thesis: We're watching OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon — it's the one most likely to affect vendor and roadmap decisions this quarter.
editors_note.hook: We're flagging the lead for leadership — it's the one most likely to shift budgets or priorities.
editors_note.spotlight: The lead story is the most likely to change near-term decisions for teams shipping AI products.
editors_note.underhyped: A Complete End-to-End Coding Guide to MLflow Experiment Tracking, Hyperparameter Optimization, Model Evaluation, and Live Model Deployment deserves more attention than it's getting.
editors_note.risk: Keep an eye on Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government — it could shift budgets or vendor assumptions.

lead_story.title: OpenAI reveals more details about its agreement with the Pentagon
lead_story.what_happened: OpenAI is making moves. Check whether your vendor assumptions still hold.
lead_story.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input.
lead_story.what_to_do: Flag OpenAI for your leadership team — it may shift near-term priorities.

quick_hits.title: Show HN: IssueScout – Find open source issues worth contributing to
quick_hits.note: IssueScout going open-source lowers barriers for experimentation and custom deployment. Why it matters: Open access to IssueScout lowers switching cost and pressures competing proprietary tools.

sections.title: Ship-It Playbooks
sections.deck: Practical implementation guides you can adapt quickly.
sections.promise: One thing to try this week.

articles.title: How to Design a Production-Grade Multi-Agent Communication System Using LangGraph Structured Message Bus, ACP Logging, and Persistent Shared State Architecture
articles.summary: New findings around ACP may shape tooling choices once validated by independent teams (via MarkTechPost).
articles.what_happened: Practical write-up on ACP — more useful than the docs alone.
articles.why_it_matters: Hands-on ACP content like this is more useful than reading the API docs alone.
articles.what_to_do: Pick the hardest step in the ACP tutorial and work backwards from there.
articles.our_take: If you can't run the example in 15 minutes, the tutorial has a friction problem, not you.

articles.title: Interactive Demo: https://coasty.ai/share/a58ee8f6-3821-4795-b4e7-4d77679a6221
articles.summary: Worth a quick read — Interactive Demo tackles a gap most teams are still working around (via Dev.to AI).
articles.what_happened: If you've been putting off Interactive Demo, this walkthrough makes a solid starting point.
articles.why_it_matters: The Interactive Demo walkthrough could save you a few hours of trial and error.
articles.what_to_do: Clone the Interactive Demo example, run it locally, then change one thing to match your project.
articles.our_take: Skip the parts you already know. The useful bit is usually two-thirds of the way through.

sections.title: Research worth your time
sections.deck: Research updates with practical product impact.

articles.title: A Complete End-to-End Coding Guide to MLflow Experiment Tracking, Hyperparameter Optimization, Model Evaluation, and Live Model Deployment
articles.summary: Concrete enough to follow along — the End Coding Guide tutorial goes beyond toy examples (via MarkTechPost).
articles.what_happened: New funding around End Coding Guide reflects investor confidence in this part of the stack.
articles.why_it_matters: Early End Coding Guide numbers look strong. Wait for independent validation before building on it.
articles.what_to_do: Compare the End Coding Guide eval setup to yours — check if the benchmark conditions match your workload.
articles.our_take: Use this for direction-setting, not for production bets. Keep it on the watch list.

articles.title: Alibaba Team Open-Sources CoPaw: A High-Performance Personal Agent Workstation for Developers to Scale Multi-Channel AI Workflows and Memory
articles.summary: Open access to Alibaba Team Open gives teams a new self-hosted option to evaluate (via MarkTechPost).
articles.what_happened: Alibaba Team Open released under an open license, giving teams full control over deployment.
articles.why_it_matters: The open release of Alibaba Team Open adds a viable alternative to proprietary options.
articles.what_to_do: Read the Alibaba Team Open abstract. If the problem matches yours, read the method. Otherwise, skip it.
articles.our_take: Don't re-architect on a single paper. Let the results mature, then re-evaluate.

sections.title: Infra
sections.deck: Cloud, scale, and infrastructure that matters to AI builders.

articles.title: OpenClaw, but in containers: Meet NanoClaw
articles.summary: Concise coverage of OpenClaw. Read it with your current stack in mind (via The Register).
articles.what_happened: New OpenClaw coverage this week — quick read with practical context.
articles.why_it_matters: No immediate action needed, but OpenClaw shifts the background assumptions a bit.
articles.what_to_do: Add OpenClaw to your weekly review and decide on a next step.
articles.our_take: Watch closely rather than act now — the picture will get clearer soon.

articles.title: SaaS-pocalypse chatter is doomster pr0n. It would be nice if enterprise IT were boring again
articles.summary: Worth a quick read — SaaS tackles a gap most teams are still working around (via The Register).
articles.what_happened: SaaS update. Not urgent, but worth knowing about.
articles.why_it_matters: SaaS sounds minor now, but it could change your shortlist by next quarter.
articles.what_to_do: Review SaaS and decide on one concrete follow-up action by end of week.
articles.our_take: Don't let FOMO drive architecture. Validate on your own terms before changing course.

sections.title: What changed in industry
sections.deck: Company moves, launches, and policy shifts that could affect your roadmap.
sections.promise: What changed and why it matters.

articles.title: Trump moves to ban Anthropic from the US government
articles.summary: Trump is getting traction for a reason. Skim the details before your next sprint (via Ars Technica AI).
articles.what_happened: New developments around Trump — relevant if it touches your stack or procurement.
articles.why_it_matters: Talent movement around Trump often precedes product or strategy pivots.
articles.what_to_do: Add Trump as a discussion item in your next strategy or planning meeting.
articles.our_take: File it now, act on it when the dust settles. Timing matters.

articles.title: OpenAI and Amazon announce strategic partnership
articles.summary: The OpenAI announcement carries implications for budgets and vendor relationships (via OpenAI Blog).
articles.what_happened: OpenAI update this week. Worth reading if you have exposure to this part of the market.
articles.why_it_matters: The OpenAI move could shift the math for your next planning cycle; flag it in leadership review.
articles.what_to_do: Monitor OpenAI for follow-on product or pricing changes over the next month.
articles.our_take: The market is sending a signal. Teams that read it early get a head start.

learn_this_week.title: Machine Learning Specialization
learn_this_week.note: Andrew Ng's refreshed ML curriculum. The gold standard intro. Audit free, cert paid.

prompt_of_the_week.text: Generate a week's worth of social media posts about [topic] in the voice of a technical thought leader — concise, opinionated, no fluff.
prompt_of_the_week.why: Technical audiences punish generic content. Specifying 'opinionated, no fluff' constrains the model away from safe, bland takes — the output reads more like a real practitioner's voice.
prompt_of_the_week.tip: Replace [topic] with your area. Add 2-3 example posts you admire for tone matching.
